Container.Name: wealthfolio
Container.Overview:
Wealthfolio is a beautiful, open-source, local-first personal finance tracker. Track investments, net worth, spending, and simulations across multiple accounts and asset types — all stored in your own SQLite database with no cloud dependency.
REQUIRED CONFIG: 1. WF_SECRET_KEY — generate with: openssl rand -base64 32 (back this up!) 2. WF_AUTH_PASSWORD_HASH — Argon2id PHC hash of your login password Generate with: printf 'your-password' | argon2 yoursalt16chars! -id -e 3. WF_CORS_ALLOW_ORIGINS — the exact URL you'll use to reach the app (e.g. http://192.168.1.10:8088 or https://wealthfolio.example.com)
Full guide: https://wealthfolio.app/docs/guide/self-hosting
Container.Description: Wealthfolio is a beautiful, local-first personal finance tracker — investments, net worth, spending, and simulations — all stored in your own SQLite database with no cloud dependency.

Config.Name: WebUI Port
Config.Description: Host port to expose. Change if 8088 clashes with another container.

Config.Name: Appdata
Config.Description: Holds the SQLite database (wealthfolio.db) and the encrypted secrets file. BACK THIS UP.

Config.Name: WF_SECRET_KEY
Config.Description: REQUIRED. 32-byte key used to encrypt secrets at rest and sign JWTs. Generate ONCE with: openssl rand -base64 32 — then save it somewhere safe. Losing it means losing all stored API keys and broker credentials.

Config.Name: WF_AUTH_PASSWORD_HASH
Config.Description: REQUIRED unless WF_AUTH_REQUIRED=false. Argon2id PHC hash of your login password. Generate with: printf 'your-password' | argon2 yoursalt16chars! -id -e — then paste the full $argon2id$... output here.

Config.Name: WF_CORS_ALLOW_ORIGINS
Config.Description: REQUIRED when auth is enabled. Comma-separated list of origins allowed to call the API. Must EXACTLY match the URL in your browser's address bar (scheme + host + port). Example: http://192.168.1.10:8088 or https://wealthfolio.example.com

Config.Name: WF_AUTH_REQUIRED
Config.Description: Set to 'false' if a reverse proxy handles authentication (in which case leave WF_AUTH_PASSWORD_HASH empty).

Config.Name: WF_AUTH_TOKEN_TTL_MINUTES
Config.Description: JWT access token lifetime in minutes.

Config.Name: WF_REQUEST_TIMEOUT_MS
Config.Description: HTTP request timeout in milliseconds. Default is 5 minutes — generous to accommodate large broker syncs.

Config.Name: WF_LISTEN_ADDR
Config.Description: Bind address inside the container. Leave at 0.0.0.0:8088 unless you know what you're doing.

Config.Name: WF_DB_PATH
Config.Description: SQLite database path inside the container. Must live under /data so it persists.
